  1. ✈️ Day 16: Departure from Brindisi (Jun 20)
  2. Day 16: Departure from Brindisi

Day 16: Departure from Brindisi

Homeward Bound — Brindisi to Madison via Munich and Chicago

itinerary
travel
departure
brindisi
Published

June 20, 2026

Overview

The journey home begins in Puglia. After 15 days exploring Florence, Naples, and the heel of Italy’s boot, we fly out of Brindisi (BDS) this morning. Three flights get us home to Madison by 10:27pm.

Today’s Flights:

# Flight Route Departs Arrives Cabin
1 LH 1895 (Air Dolomiti) BDS → MUC 10:55am 12:45pm Economy
2 UA 8855 (Lufthansa) MUC → ORD 3:40pm 6:15pm Premium Economy
3 UA 1400 ORD → MSN 9:15pm 10:27pm Premium Economy

Reservation Code: FMWYOJ

Morning: Brindisi Airport (8:00–10:55am)

Getting to the Airport

Brindisi Airport (BDS) — Papola Casale Airport - Located ~4 km from Brindisi center - Short taxi ride from hotel (~10–15 minutes, €10–15) - Allow 2.5 hours before departure for international check-in

Depart hotel by: 8:15am at the latest

Check-In

  • Check in online 24 hours before (Lufthansa app or lufthansa.com)
  • Have passport ready
  • Check baggage allowance: 2x23kg checked + 1x8kg carry-on per person
  • Seats will be assigned at check-in (assigned seating requires check-in)

Brindisi Airport Notes

  • Small regional airport — no large terminal mall
  • One departure hall, straightforward security
  • Limited food/coffee options airside — eat breakfast at hotel before leaving
  • Duty-free is minimal

Flight 1: Brindisi → Munich (LH 1895)

Operated by: Air Dolomiti (regional Lufthansa partner) Aircraft: Embraer 195 Jet Duration: 1 hour 50 minutes Cabin: Economy Meals: Refreshment / Food & Beverage for Purchase

  • Departs BDS 10:55am → Arrives MUC 12:45pm (Terminal 2)
  • Compact regional jet — overhead bins fill fast, board early
  • No seat assignment until check-in
  • This short hop gets you into Lufthansa’s main Munich hub

Munich Layover (12:45pm–3:40pm) — ~3 Hours

Munich Airport (MUC) — Terminal 2

Stay in Terminal 2 — both MUC flights use T2 (Lufthansa hub). No terminal changes needed.

Layover checklist: - [ ] Clear EU passport control (if not already cleared in BDS — likely just a transit) - [ ] Confirm UA 8855 departure gate on boards - [ ] Have lunch at Munich airport — excellent options in T2 (Bavarian food, good coffee) - [ ] Browse duty-free if desired - [ ] Find departure gate before 3:15pm boarding call

Time to kill: ~3 hours is comfortable. Don’t leave the secured area.

Flight 2: Munich → Chicago (UA 8855)

Operated by: Lufthansa for United Aircraft: Airbus A359 (widebody long-haul) Duration: 9 hours 35 minutes Cabin: Premium Economy Meals: Full meal service included

  • Departs MUC 3:40pm (T2) → Arrives ORD 6:15pm (International Terminal 5)
  • Premium Economy: wider seats, more legroom, dedicated cabin
  • 9.5-hour transatlantic crossing — longest leg of the day
  • Tips: sleep if possible, stay hydrated, walk the aisle every 2 hours

Arriving Chicago: US Customs and Immigration at Terminal 5 (international arrivals) - Fill out/confirm customs declaration - Collect checked bags at T5 baggage claim - Clear customs (“Nothing to Declare” if applicable) - Recheck bags for domestic connection — bags must be rechecked after customs

Chicago O’Hare Domestic Connection (6:15pm–9:15pm) — ~3 Hours

After clearing customs at Terminal 5:

  1. Collect bags, clear customs
  2. Recheck bags at United check-in for MSN flight (domestic counter, Terminal 1)
  3. Take the ATS train to Terminal 1 (United domestic)
  4. Re-clear security (TSA)
  5. Find gate for UA 1400 to Madison

Time: 3-hour connection is comfortable for international-to-domestic at ORD. Don’t panic — customs can be 30–45 minutes, but you have margin.

Food: ORD Terminal 1 has many dining options. Grab dinner before the final hop.

Flight 3: Chicago → Madison (UA 1400)

Aircraft: Boeing 737-700 Duration: 1 hour 12 minutes Cabin: Premium Economy Seats: 08D (Dan) / 08E (Jane) Meals: No meal service

  • Departs ORD 9:15pm (Terminal 1) → Arrives MSN 10:27pm
  • Short final leg home
  • You’re almost there!

Arrival: Madison (10:27pm)

Welcome home. After 16 days, 3 Italian cities, and countless memories, you land in Madison just after 10:30pm.

After landing: - Short walk to baggage claim at Dane County Regional Airport - Ground transportation: taxi, rideshare, or pre-arranged pickup - Don’t drive if you’re exhausted — consider a taxi/Uber home

Jet lag note: You’ve been in Italy (UTC+2 in June). Madison is UTC-5/CDT. That’s a 7-hour difference. Expect to feel it for 2–3 days.

Packing Up for Departure

The Night Before (June 19)

Souvenirs & Luggage

  • Puglia olive oil and ceramics pack well if wrapped carefully
  • Declare any food items to US Customs (dried goods often OK; fresh produce not)
  • Keep receipts for any expensive purchases (VAT refund if applicable)

Reflections

Space for end-of-trip notes

Favorite moment of the trip:

Best meal:

Place I’d most like to return to:

What surprised me most:

For next time:

Previous Day: Day 15: Puglia

Buon viaggio di ritorno! 🇮🇹 → 🇺🇸

Italy Trip 2026 - Columbus
